DocuSign in charts: Subscription revenue maintains single-digit growth pace in Q3; Billings up 10.3% Y/Y
Netflix puts up top bid for Warner Bros Discovery - reports
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) emerged as the highest bidder for Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD), according to a Reuters report that cites sources familiar with the matter.

Another Bloomberg report also suggested that Netflix (NFLX) had entered exclusive talks to buy WBD's (WBD) studio and streaming service and is offering a $5 billion breakup fee if regulators don't approve the deal. The report also indicated that the deal could be announced in five days.

Netflix and Warner Bros. Discovery did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

Previous day, a CNBC report suggested the same, with the Netflix offer consisting of 85% cash.

The acquisition would consolidate Netflix's (NFLX) dominance in streaming by absorbing Warner Bros. (WBD) studios and HBO Max, potentially creating bundled offerings to cut consumer costs. WBD (WBD) initiated the sale process in October amid financial pressures.

data.title: Hewlett Packard in charts: Networking revenue sees highest Y/Y rise of 150.2% in FQ4
Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
08:30 AM Personal Income and Outlays 
 
Personal income represents the income that households receive from all sources, including wages and salaries, fringe benefits such as employer contributions to private pension plans, proprietors' income, income from rent, dividends, and interest, and transfer payments. 
 
Income and spending are both expected to be up a robust 0.4 percent on the month. PCE prices are seen up 0.3 percent on the month for total and core.
 
10:00 AM Consumer Sentiment 
 
The University of Michigan's Consumer Survey Center questions households each month on their assessment of current conditions and expectations of future conditions.
 
Not a pretty picture for consumer sentiment, with 52.0 expected for the first read on December versus an abysmal 51.0 in the November final.
10:00 AM Factory Orders 
 
Factory orders represent the dollar level of new orders for both durable and nondurable goods. This report gives more complete information than the advance durable goods report, which is released one or two weeks earlier in the month.
1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count 
 
The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.
 
3:00 PM Consumer Credit 
 
The dollar value of consumer installment credit outstanding. Changes in consumer credit indicate the state of consumer finances and portend future spending patterns. 
 
A trendlike rise of $9.4 billion is the call for October.

data.title: Stitch Fix raises full-year revenue outlook to $1.32B–$1.35B amid accelerating client engagement and AI-driven innovation
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Stitch Fix (SFIX) Q1 2026

Management View

  • Matt Baer, CEO, reported "Q1 was a strong start to the year. Revenue exceeded our outlook and accelerated 7.3% year-over-year to $342.1 million. Adjusted EBITDA also exceeded our outlook and was nearly 4% of revenue at $13.4 million." He emphasized leveraging generative AI technology, human stylists, and a diverse brand assortment to deliver a highly personalized shopping experience.
  • Baer highlighted four key areas of focus: enhanced client engagement features, deeper client-stylist relationships, best-in-class assortment, and increased business model flexibility. He noted, "Our fix AOV was up nearly 10% in Q1, the ninth consecutive quarter AOV has increased year-over-year as our larger fix offerings and our improved assortment continue to resonate with our clients and better meet their outfitting needs."
  • The company is gaining market share in core apparel, with strategic expansion into non-apparel segments. Baer stated, "Our 7.3% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 meaningfully outpaced Circana's estimated 1% growth for the broader U.S. apparel, accessories and footwear market."
  • Growth was described as broad-based, with both women's and men's businesses accelerating. Women's fall sales in key seasonal categories grew 19% year-over-year, and sneakers rose 63%. Men's seasonal categories grew 57%, denim 30%, and sneakers 24%. Baer said, "We believe that our expanded relevance in activewear and athleisure, footwear and accessories, in particular, could unlock a significant wallet share opportunity and that our fair share with our existing client base in these categories is approximately $1 billion of incremental revenue."
  • Baer confirmed a focus on profitable active client growth, ending the quarter with 2.3 million active clients. He reported, "Q1 marked the sixth consecutive quarter of improvement in active client year-over-year growth rates and a return to sequential active client growth in our men's business."
  • CFO David Aufderhaar stated, "We delivered a strong first quarter that underscores the success of our strategy and the momentum Matt outlined. We're accelerating growth and gaining market share while maintaining financial discipline to ensure that growth is profitable and sustainable."

Outlook

  • For full year FY '26, Stitch Fix raised guidance, expecting total revenue between $1.32 billion and $1.35 billion and adjusted EBITDA between $38 million and $48 million. Free cash flow is projected to be positive for the year.
  • Q2 revenue is expected between $335 million and $340 million with adjusted EBITDA between $10 million and $13 million.
  • Aufderhaar commented, "The resilience in client demand that we saw in Q1 and through the first part of Q2 gives us confidence to guide to another quarter of growth acceleration in Q2 and to raise our guidance for the full year in FY '26."
  • The company expects active client year-over-year growth rates to continue improving in Q2, with a sequential increase in net adds in Q3 FY '26.

Financial Results

  • Q1 revenue was $342.1 million, up 7.3% year-over-year. Average order value rose 9.6%, driven by more items per fix and higher average unit retail. Revenue per active client reached $559, up 5.3% year-over-year.
  • Gross margin was 43.6%. Contribution margin remained above 30% for the seventh straight quarter. Advertising was 9.9% of revenue in Q1.
  • Q1 adjusted EBITDA was $13.4 million or 3.9% margin. The company ended Q1 with $244.2 million in cash and short-term investments and no debt. Inventory was $141.5 million, up 18.8% year-over-year.

Q&A

  • Marcus Bellinger (William Blair) asked about new customer behavior and stickiness. CEO Baer responded that new client LTVs have improved for nine consecutive quarters, and reengaged clients are staying longer and spending more. "We just completed a quarter in which we had the lowest number of dormant clients in 5 years."
  • Aneesha Sherman (Bernstein) inquired about market share gains and gifting trends. Baer said, "We're picking up share from where other retailers aren't delivering... we're hearing loud and clear from the brands that we work with, that we are delivering outsized growth relative to others that they might be working with." On gifting, the recent launch of family accounts exceeded expectations and drove strong engagement.
  • Sherman also asked about advertising. CFO Aufderhaar explained, "This quarter, we did lean in a little bit on advertising. We ended up at the high end of that 9% to 10% range at 9.9%." He noted new client LTV was up almost 17% year-over-year.
  • David Bellinger (Mizuho) asked about early adoption of AI and visualization tools. Baer said engagement with Stitch Fix Vision "far exceeded our expectations" and highlighted its impact on client acquisition and social sharing. Bellinger also queried gross margin. Aufderhaar attributed the decline to higher transportation expenses, investments in lower-margin categories like footwear, and tariffs, but emphasized, "having contribution margins that are still well over 30%, they were 32.5% this last quarter."
  • Jay Sole (UBS) questioned brand partnerships and RPAC drivers. Baer described strong brand attraction to Stitch Fix's closed ecosystem and high client engagement. Aufderhaar explained RPAC growth was due to higher new client LTVs, increased order values, and a higher number of items per fix.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on detailed metrics behind client growth, market share, and the efficacy of new features, showing a tone that was generally positive and inquisitive, with some probing for sustainability and margin details.
  • Management consistently expressed confidence and enthusiasm, frequently citing long-term growth, client engagement, and innovation as core drivers. Baer noted, "We are confident in our ability to capture increased market share in the future."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained an assertive and optimistic tone, now bolstered by raised guidance. Analysts' tone remained positive but included more specific queries on sustainability and the impact of new initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company raised full-year revenue and EBITDA guidance from the previous quarter, reflecting confidence in ongoing momentum. Q1 revenue growth accelerated to 7.3% year-over-year from the prior quarter’s 4.4%.
  • Both quarters highlighted expanding market share, but the current quarter emphasized faster revenue growth, higher AOV, and a rebound in active client growth for men's.
  • Management’s tone remained confident, with added emphasis on innovation and AI-driven engagement. Analysts shifted from broad strategy to granular questions on market share sources and new client metrics.
  • Strategic priorities—AI, assortment expansion, and client engagement—remained consistent, but current remarks focused more on leveraging these for near-term growth and profitability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted headwinds from consumer confidence and inflation impacting discretionary spending, cautioning that tougher AOV comps are expected in the second half of FY '26.
  • Gross margin pressures were attributed to higher transportation costs, investments in lower-margin categories, and minor tariff impacts. Aufderhaar stressed ongoing efficiency efforts to mitigate these.
  • Analysts probed for sustainability of current growth rates and the impact of new client acquisition and advertising strategies.

Final Takeaway

Stitch Fix’s management highlighted a strong start to fiscal 2026, with momentum in revenue and client engagement driven by AI-powered personalization and expansion in both core and non-apparel categories. The company raised its full-year outlook, pointing to continued gains in market share and a focus on sustainable, profitable growth. Management credited disciplined execution, innovation, and a strengthened operating model as key to capturing increased wallet share and delivering long-term value for clients and shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
